info.title: IDaaS – Identity as a Service API
info.description:
## Overview
IDaaS provides globally unique, portable identity **tags** that travel with users across applications.  Each tag is a short handle (e.g. `@alice`) that can carry verified claims, participate in cross-application payments, and be federated to external identity providers.

## Key Capabilities
- **Tags** – create, transfer, disable, and attach claims to identity handles.
- **Cross-application payments** – send money from any tag on Application A   to any tag on Application B; each application has a single escrow wallet   tracked for end-of-day settlement.
- **Ledger** – every completed payment produces an immutable double-entry   `DEBIT` / `CREDIT` pair recorded against application wallets.
- **Webhooks** – real-time callbacks with HMAC-SHA256 signatures and   automatic retry (up to 3 attempts with exponential back-off).
- **Settlement** – a scheduled end-of-day job aggregates daily wallet   movements and emits `SETTLEMENT_BATCH` Kafka events.

## Authentication
1. Register an application: `POST /v1/applications`
2. Exchange credentials for a JWT: `POST /v1/auth/token`
3. Include the JWT as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on all protected calls.

## Rate Limits
| Endpoint | Limit |
|---|---|
| `POST /v1/auth/token` | 10 req / min per IP |
| `POST /v1/transactions` | 60 req / min per application |
| All other | 300 req / min per IP |

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Exchange client credentials for a JWT access token.

tags.name: Claims
tags.description: Attach and revoke verifiable claims on tags.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Exchange credentials for access tokens and rotate integration credentials

tags.name: Encryption
tags.description:
JWE payload encryption – end-to-end security for API request bodies and webhook payloads.
Each application receives an EC P-256 keypair at registration:
- IDaaS encrypts **outbound webhooks** with the application's public key.
- Applications encrypt **inbound request bodies** with the IDaaS public key.
Algorithm: ECDH-ES+A256KW + A256GCM (JSON Web Encryption, RFC 7516).
Use `GET /v1/keys/idaas` to fetch the IDaaS public key.
Use `POST /v1/applications/keys/rotate` to rotate your application keypair.

tags.name: Settlement
tags.description: Query end-of-day settlement batches and per-application net positions.

tags.name: Subjects
tags.description: Manage the real-world entities (users / organisations) behind tags.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Inspect outbound webhook delivery records for transactions. Each transaction has at most two webhook records: one RECEIVER (sent on initiation) and one SENDER (sent on acceptance/rejection/expiry).

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Privileged cross-entity administration endpoints for operators.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Inspect outbound webhook delivery records (RECEIVER and SENDER) for transactions.

tags.name: Wallet
tags.description: View application escrow wallet balance and paginated ledger statement.

tags.name: Wallet
tags.description: View application escrow wallet balance and paginated ledger statement (DEBIT / CREDIT entries).

tags.name: Encryption
tags.description: Public key distribution and keypair management for JWE payload encryption.

tags.name: Consent
tags.description: Issue and manage user consent tokens for claim federation.

tags.name: Transactions
tags.description: Initiate, accept/reject, and query cross-application tag-to-tag payments.

tags.name: Applications
tags.description: Register and manage API client applications (tenants).

tags.name: Tags
tags.description: Create, transfer, disable and resolve globally unique identity tags.

get.description:
Returns the current escrow wallet for the authenticated application including:
- `balance` – net position (credits − debits for all completed transactions)
- `totalDebited` – running sum of all outgoing accepted payments
- `totalCredited` – running sum of all incoming accepted payments

The wallet is auto-provisioned on the first transaction so calling this endpoint
before any transaction is initiated will return a 404 until the wallet exists.
Call `POST /v1/wallet` to provision it eagerly.

404.description: Wallet not yet provisioned for this application

post.description: Idempotent – safe to call on every application start-up. Creates the wallet if it does not exist and returns the current state.

Creates a new cross-application payment from a tag owned by the calling application
to any tag on any application.  The transaction starts in `AWAITING_ACCEPTANCE` state.

The receiving application is notified via webhook and must call
`POST /v1/transactions/{reference}/accept` or `.../reject`.

**Idempotency**: Supply a unique `Idempotency-Key` header; duplicate calls within
24 hours return the original response without creating a new transaction.

**Rate limited to 60 requests per minute per application.**

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional idempotency key (UUID or any unique string, max 128 chars). Duplicate calls with the same key within 24 hours return the original response.

403.description: Sender tag does not belong to the calling application

400.description: Validation error (invalid tags, zero amount, etc.)

post.description:
The **receiving application** calls this to reject an `AWAITING_ACCEPTANCE` transaction.

No ledger movement occurs on rejection – nothing was debited at initiation.
Transaction status moves to `REJECTED`.
Sender is notified via webhook callback URL with the rejection reason.

409.description: Transaction is not in AWAITING_ACCEPTANCE state

403.description: Calling application is not the receiver

post.description:
The **receiving application** calls this to accept an `AWAITING_ACCEPTANCE` transaction.

On acceptance:
- Sender application's escrow wallet is **debited**.
- Receiver application's escrow wallet is **credited**.
- Two immutable `LedgerEntry` rows are written (DEBIT + CREDIT).
- Transaction status moves to `COMPLETED`.
- Sender is notified via webhook callback URL.

409.description: Transaction is not in AWAITING_ACCEPTANCE state or has expired

403.description: Calling application is not the receiver

get.description: Returns all tags created by the authenticated application.

post.description: Creates a globally unique tag for a subject owned by the authenticated application.
Transfers a tag from the current application-owned subject to another target subject.

get.description: Retrieves federated identity linkage details for the specified tag.

post.description: Starts federated identity linking for a tag by issuing a challenge for cross-application verification.

get.description: Returns all claims currently recorded for a tag, scoped to the authenticated application context.

post.description: Requests a claim to link the requesting application's subject identity to an existing tag.

get.description: Lists all subjects that belong to the authenticated application.

post.description: Creates a new subject within the authenticated application's tenant scope.

Runs the end-of-day settlement for a specified date on demand.
This is idempotent – if the date has already been successfully settled,
the existing record is returned without reprocessing.

**Use cases**:
- Re-running a failed batch (e.g. after a database outage).
- Settling a back-date after delayed transaction processing.
- Smoke-testing the settlement pipeline in staging.

The scheduled job automatically covers yesterday at 23:59 UTC;
this endpoint is for operational override only.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Date to settle (ISO-8601 format, e.g. 2025-03-03). Defaults to yesterday UTC if omitted.

500.description: Settlement run failed – error details in response

200.description: Settlement run completed (or already completed for this date)

post.description: Verifies a federation challenge token and finalizes cross-application identity linking.

409.description: Challenge token is not in a verifiable state

404.description: Challenge token not found or expired

post.description: Denies a pending consent token and marks the related claim request as rejected.

404.description: Consent token not found or expired

post.description: Approves a pending consent token and returns an attestation token for the requesting application.

404.description: Consent token not found or expired

200.description: Consent approved and attestation token issued

post.description: Revokes a previously approved claim for the authenticated application so it is no longer considered valid.

409.description: Claim is not in a revocable state

post.description:
Issues a signed HS256 JWT access token valid for the configured TTL (default 1 hour).
Accepts either `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` query parameters or a JSON body
with `client_id` and `client_secret` fields.

**Rate limited to 10 requests per minute per IP.**

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Client ID (query param or JSON field)

parameters.name: client_secret
parameters.description: Client secret (query param or JSON field)

post.summary: Rotate the calling application's webhook signing secret
post.description:
Generates a new webhook signing secret for the authenticated application,
stores only its hash, and returns the raw secret once.

Use the returned `webhookSecret` to verify `X-IDaaS-Signature` on inbound webhooks.
The previous webhook secret becomes invalid immediately.

200.description: Webhook secret rotated - new secret returned once

post.summary: Rotate the calling application's EC keypair
post.description:
Generates a fresh EC P-256 keypair for the authenticated application and
returns the new public key (JWK) and private key material (`appPrivateJwk`).

**The private JWK is returned only once** - store it securely.
After rotation:
- IDaaS will encrypt all outbound webhooks with the **new** public key.
- Requests encrypted with the **old** public key will be rejected (Content-Encryption: JWE decryption will fail).
- Allow a brief migration window for in-flight messages before destroying the old private key.

The `keyVersion` field is incremented on each rotation.

200.description: Keypair rotated - new key material returned

patch.description: Disables a tag owned by the authenticated application so it can no longer be used for new operations.

get.description: Returns one subject by UUID.

patch.description: Applies partial updates to a subject record owned by the authenticated application.

get.summary: Get a single webhook record by UUID
get.description: Returns the full webhook delivery record including payload JSON and all attempt history.

get.summary: List all FAILED webhooks for the calling application
get.description:
Returns all webhook delivery records in `FAILED` status where the
target URL belongs to the calling application.  Use this to identify
events that need to be replayed after fixing an endpoint outage.
Paginated, newest first.

200.description: Paginated list of FAILED webhook records

get.description:
Returns DEBIT and CREDIT entries for the calling application's wallet in
reverse-chronological order.  Each entry includes:
- `entryType` – `DEBIT` (outgoing) or `CREDIT` (incoming)
- `amount`, `currency`
- `openingBalance` / `closingBalance` – wallet balance before/after the movement
- `description` – human-readable summary referencing the transaction
- `createdAt` – when the ledger line was written
Retrieves the full transaction details including status, webhook delivery status, and timestamps.

get.summary: List all webhook records for a transaction
get.description:
Returns all outbound webhook delivery records for a transaction,
ordered by creation time.  Typically returns 0–2 records:
- `RECEIVER` – notification to the receiving application on initiation.
- `SENDER`   – callback to the sending application on outcome.
Each record shows the delivery status, attempt count, last error,
and the full event type sent.
Returns the outbound callback record for the sending application (posted after acceptance/rejection/expiry).

404.description: Transaction or sender webhook not yet created (outcome not yet determined)

get.description: Returns the outbound webhook record for the receiving application (TRANSACTION_INITIATED notification).

404.description: Transaction or webhook record not found

get.summary: List transactions sent by the calling application
get.description: Returns all transactions where the sender tag belongs to the calling application. Sorted by `createdAt` descending.

get.summary: List transactions received by the calling application
get.description: Returns all transactions where the receiver tag belongs to the calling application. Sorted by `createdAt` descending.
Returns transactions addressed to the calling application that are in `AWAITING_ACCEPTANCE` state. These require an explicit accept or reject action.
Resolves a tag to its canonical profile and ownership details. This endpoint is public.
Searches tags by keyword and optionally narrows results to a specific application handle.
Returns all tags currently linked to a subject.
Looks up a subject by the external identifier in the authenticated application's namespace.

Returns a paginated list of settlement batches in reverse-chronological order.
Each record contains aggregate totals; individual per-application entries are
omitted in the list view for performance. Use `GET /v1/settlement/{id}` for
full details including per-application net positions.

get.summary: Get a settlement batch by ID
get.description: Returns the full settlement record including all per-application net position entries.

get.summary: Get a settlement batch by date
get.description: Returns the settlement record for a specific calendar date (ISO-8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DD`).

404.description: No settlement found for the specified date

get.summary: Get the IDaaS public encryption key
get.description:
Returns the IDaaS EC P-256 public key in two formats:
- **`jwk`** – JSON Web Key (JWK) format; import directly into any JOSE library.
- **`publicKeyBase64`** – Base64-encoded X.509 (SubjectPublicKeyInfo) DER; use with
  `KeyFactory.getInstance("EC").generatePublic(new X509EncodedKeySpec(decoded))` in Java.

Use this key to **encrypt request payloads** before sending them to IDaaS:
```
1. Serialize your JSON request body.
2. Encrypt with JWE (alg=ECDH-ES+A256KW, enc=A256GCM) using this public key.
3. Set header: Content-Encryption: JWE
4. Send the JWE compact string as the request body.
```

This endpoint is **unauthenticated** – public keys are safe to distribute.
Cache the response; refresh only if you encounter a JWE decryption error (HTTP 400).
Returns consent request details for a consent token so a user can review and decide to approve or deny.

404.description: Consent token not found or expired

delete.description: Revokes an existing federated identity link owned by the authenticated application.

senderTag.description: The sender's tag handle (must belong to the calling application)

receiverTag.description: The recipient's tag handle (can belong to any application)

amount.description: Positive payment amount with up to 4 decimal places

callbackUrl.description: Optional HTTPS URL that IDaaS will POST the final transaction status to once accepted or rejected

idempotencyKey.description: Client-supplied idempotency key (UUID v4 recommended). Duplicate calls within 24 hours return the original response.

InitiateTransactionRequest.description: Request body for initiating a cross-application tag-to-tag payment

reference.description: Human-readable unique reference for the transaction

expiresAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp after which the transaction auto-expires if not accepted

acceptedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the transaction was accepted (null until COMPLETED)

rejectedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the transaction was rejected (null unless REJECTED)

rejectionReason.description: Rejection reason supplied by the receiving application (null unless REJECTED)

webhooks.description: Outbound webhook delivery records (RECEIVER and/or SENDER). Included in single-transaction GET; omitted in list responses.

TransactionResponse.description: Full details of a cross-application tag-to-tag payment transaction

applicationId.description: UUID of the target application (receiver or sender)

targetUrl.description: HTTP endpoint that was (or will be) called

eventType.description: Event name included in the webhook body

attempts.description: Total number of HTTP delivery attempts made (max 3)

lastError.description: HTTP status code or exception message from the most recent attempt

lastAttemptAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent delivery attempt

deliveredAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when delivery was confirmed (HTTP 2xx). Null until delivered.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 creation timestamp (when the webhook was first queued)

TransactionWebhookResponse.description: Outbound webhook delivery record for a transaction

walletId.description: UUID of the application's escrow wallet

totalCredited.description: Total incoming accepted payments for the settlement period

totalDebited.description: Total outgoing accepted payments for the settlement period

netAmount.description: Net position (totalCredited − totalDebited). Positive = net creditor.

openingBalance.description: Wallet balance at the start of the settlement date

closingBalance.description: Wallet balance at the end of the settlement date

transactionCount.description: Number of completed transactions included in this entry

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this entry was created

SettlementEntryResponse.description: Per-application net position within an end-of-day settlement batch

settlementDate.description: Calendar date (UTC) covered by this settlement batch

totalCredited.description: Total amount credited across all applications on this date

totalDebited.description: Total amount debited across all applications on this date

transactionCount.description: Total count of completed transactions included in this batch

applicationCount.description: Number of application wallets included in this batch

errorMessage.description: Error message if the batch failed (null when COMPLETED)

SettlementRecordResponse.description: End-of-day settlement batch aggregating all completed transaction movements

appHandle.description: Short unique slug used as the tag namespace for this application (e.g. 'walletapp'). Tags are addressed as 'localTag@appHandle' across the platform. Immutable once set.

clientSecret.description: Raw client secret – returned ONLY on first registration; never stored in plaintext

active.description: Whether this application can still use IDaaS

redirectUris.description: Space-separated list of allowed redirect URIs

transactionWebhookUrl.description: URL that IDaaS POSTs transaction notifications to

webhookSecret.description: Raw webhook signing secret – returned ONLY on first registration. Used to verify X-IDaaS-Signature headers on inbound webhooks via HMAC-SHA256.

logoUrl.description: Optional URL of the application logo image

encryptionEnabled.description: Whether JWE payload encryption is enabled for this application. When true, IDaaS requires encrypted inbound request bodies and encrypts outbound webhooks.

keyVersion.description: Current keypair version. Incremented on each rotation.

JwkDto.description: EC P-256 private key in JWK format (includes 'd'). Returned ONLY on registration and on key rotation. Store securely – this can decrypt payloads encrypted for your application.

bearerAuth.description: Obtain a token from `POST /v1/auth/token` using your `client_id` and `client_secret`, then enter `Bearer <token>` here.
